body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready
route: record (RECORDED 2026-05-26)

## Scenario

session-reset: a session runs a turn, then the user invokes a `/new` reset WITHIN the same running process; the old session row disappears and a fresh session_id is born in ready. Distinct from session-end + session-start (separate processes/invocations).

## Agent supports it (agent_supports: yes)

opencode's interactive TUI ships a `/new` command (binary 1.14.50 carries `session.new` / a `New session` command-palette entry). In a live TUI the user resets the conversation to a fresh `ses_…` mid-session — exactly this scenario.

## daemon observes it (daemon_capability: full)

opencode is the lone `ProcessOwnedStore` adapter (`core/adapters/inbound/agents/opencode/agent.go`): the daemon reads session state from the SQLite store. The committed recording proves the full arc live — a transient `proc-<PID>` pre-session hands off to the first `ses_…` (turn 1 working→ready), then `/new` mints a SECOND `ses_…` (`transcript_new`), the daemon RETIRES the old row (`transcript_removed` for the first ses_… — the 'old session disappears' signal, which opencode emits and codex's `/clear` does NOT), turn 2 runs working→ready on the new session, then `process_exited`. expected-validate passes 7/7 phases.

## Driver: now ready (was gap:slash-command)

The blocker was the driver, not the agent or daemon. Headless `opencode run` treats a slash command as ordinary prompt text (PROBED 2026-05-26: `opencode run "/new"` minted ONE ses_… and stored `/new` as a user message). `drive-opencode-interactive.sh` now routes any recipe carrying a `reset_session` (or `slash`) step to a LIVE-TUI path: it launches the opencode TUI under tmux, sends prompts via `tmux send-keys`, detects turn completion by polling the SQLite store for a new `step-finish` part, and delivers `/new` as a real in-REPL command. The fresh `ses_…` is minted lazily on the next prompt (same as the initial session, and codex's post-/clear rollout); the driver enumerates both top-level `ses_…` rows for the run cwd and emits the multi-session contract (`session.uuids` / `transcript.paths`) so curate keeps both. All other opencode cells keep the simpler, deterministic headless path.

## Confidence

0.9: empirically recorded and validated (7/7 expected phases; replay golden regenerated). The full supersession arc — including the old-session `transcript_removed` — is captured in events.jsonl.
caveats: The reset minting is lazy: opencode creates the new `ses_…` row on the first prompt AFTER `/new`, not on `/new` itself (the recipe's post-reset send is what materializes it). This mirrors the initial session and codex's post-/clear rollout.

Live-TUI cell: the driver routes session-reset (and any future reset_session/slash recipe) through a tmux-driven opencode TUI because headless `opencode run` cannot deliver in-REPL slash commands. tmux must be on PATH at record time.

Stronger than codex/pi supersession: opencode DOES emit `transcript_removed` for the retired old session (same-PID cleanup), so the 'old session disappears' bullet is directly observable here, not just inferred from the new session appearing.

sources.note: live-TUI path (run_live): tmux send-keys for prompts, step-finish polling for wait_turn, `/new` for reset_session, multi-session session.uuids/transcript.paths contract; routed in when the recipe carries a reset_session/slash step
recorded arc: proc-<PID> pre-session → ses_1 handoff → turn 1 → transcript_new ses_2 (/new reset) → transcript_removed ses_1 (old session retired) → turn 2 → process_exited
opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter; daemon reads session state from SQLite, so a fresh /new-spawned ses_… is observed in ready

sources.ref: opencode TUI under tmux + opencode run "/new" (2026-05-26)
sources.note: TUI /new mints a second ses_… under the same process on the next prompt (RECORDABLE); headless `opencode run "/new"` stores /new as user text (NOT a reset) — why the live-TUI path is required
TUI ships a /new reset (session.new / New session command) — agent_supports:yes
coverage cell should update to agent_supports:yes, daemon_capability:full, driver_capability:ready (was supports=unknown / recording deferred)
